name: Grilled Swordfish Recipe with a Mediterranean Twist
description: Easy grilled swordfish recipe that utilizes a delicious Mediterranean marinade with cumin, fresh garlic cloves and more. But you don't even need to leave it in the marinade too long! Just a few minutes while your grill is heating!
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp fresh lemon juice, more for later
4 swordfish steaks, about 5 to 6 ounces each, from sustainable sources
recipeInstructions: In a food processor, blend the garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, spices, salt and pepper for about three minutes or until well-combined forming a thick and smooth marinade.
Pat the swordfish steaks dry and place them in a pan (or a dish with sides to it) and apply the marinade generously on both sides and set aside for 15 minutes or so while you heat the grill.

Preheat a gas grill on high (be sure to oil the grates before using). When ready, grill the fish steaks on high heat for 5 to 6 minutes on one side, turn over once and grill on the other side for 3 minutes or so (the fish should flake easily, while maintain firmness. You will likely see a bit of pink on the inside, but it should cook through by the time it gets to the table).
Finish with a splash of fresh lemon juice and a sprinkle of crushed red pepper flakes if you like heat. Enjoy!
author: Suzy Karadsheh

ai.cliffNotes: A garlicky Mediterranean marinade with warm spices penetrates the fish during a brief rest, then high-heat grilling creates a firm exterior while the center stays moist.
keyTips: Pat the swordfish completely dry before marinating so the fish surface can sear properly instead of steaming
Oil your grill grates thoroughly and preheat to high heat to prevent sticking and develop a golden crust
Don't overcook — swordfish is dense and forgiving, but pull it off when it flakes easily; residual heat will finish cooking as it rests
Apply the marinade generously on both sides during the 15-minute rest; this timing is just enough to flavor without the acidity breaking down the fish's texture
